description: 5-item eval set for the research orchestrator. Each item is a query plus the content criteria its report must satisfy. The shared_criteria apply to every item (report structure); an item PASSES only if every shared and item-specific criterion passes. Target: >= 4/5 items pass.
pass_rule: An item passes iff all shared_criteria AND all of its own criteria are satisfied by the generated report.
shared_criteria: Opens with a 2-4 sentence executive summary before the first section heading
Uses at least 3 markdown section headings (## ...)
Includes at least 3 inline citations written as [text](url)
Ends with a '## Sources' section listing the referenced URLs
Is roughly one page (~400-1200 words): substantive but not bloated

items.query: compare Pinecone vs Weaviate for hybrid search
criteria: Substantively covers BOTH Pinecone and Weaviate, not just one
Addresses hybrid search specifically (sparse/BM25 + dense vector fusion), not generic vector similarity

items.query: compare REST vs gRPC for internal microservice communication
criteria: Names at least one concrete tradeoff dimension (e.g. performance/streaming, schema + codegen, browser support, or human-readability)

items.query: when should you choose retrieval-augmented generation over fine-tuning
criteria: Gives decision criteria for choosing one over the other (e.g. data freshness, cost, domain adaptation, hallucination control)

items.query: what are the main security risks of connecting to third-party MCP servers
criteria: Identifies at least two distinct risk categories (e.g. prompt injection, credential/token exposure, over-broad tool permissions, supply-chain trust)
Suggests at least one concrete mitigation

items.query: how do vector databases implement approximate nearest neighbor search
criteria: Names at least one concrete ANN algorithm or index (e.g. HNSW, IVF, product quantization)
Explains the accuracy-vs-speed tradeoff ANN makes versus exact nearest-neighbor search
